interpretation: IEA allocation for the U.S.; DOE had released 17.5 mb by the week ending 2026-04-24
caveats: SPR release is structured as an exchange; gross withdrawals are not the same as permanent market supply.

timing: No separate public obligation relief identified
interpretation: The U.S. line item is a strategic SPR release, not a private stockholding relaxation
caveats: Do not mix with commercial inventory changes.

interpretation: U.S. commercial crude inventories excluding SPR fell by 3.8 mb to 408.4 mb; total commercial petroleum inventories fell by 0.7 mb
caveats: This is a commercial draw, not a strategic release; it is partly seasonal and market-driven.

interpretation: Commercial crude inventories were about 7% below the five-year average
caveats: Baseline comparison only; not a policy release.

interpretation: Weekly data update cadence allows fast tracking of SPR and commercial stocks
caveats: Weekly series lag is much shorter than monthly/statistical publications.

interpretation: IEA members agreed to make 400 mb of emergency reserves available to the market
caveats: Published country table later summed to 426 mb and is explicitly subject to change; not all of this is drawn stock.

interpretation: Provisional IEA split shows 119 mb from obligated industry stocks and 280 mb from public stocks
caveats: The split is provisional; countries can refine details later.

interpretation: Global observed inventories drew by 129 mb in March, according to preliminary IEA data
caveats: Observed stock draw is not the same as reserve release; it blends policy response, demand, and routing changes.

interpretation: Global observed inventories drew by a further 117 mb in April, according to preliminary IEA data
caveats: Observed stock draw is not the same as reserve release; it blends policy response, demand, and routing changes.

interpretation: Global observed inventories fell by 143 mb in May, according to preliminary IEA data
caveats: Observed stock draw is not the same as reserve release; it blends policy response, demand, and routing changes.

interpretation: OECD government inventories fell by 163 mb over Mar-May; IEA says this is the lowest since Dec 1990
caveats: This is an observed inventory path, not a direct measure of announced releases.

interpretation: IEA Oil Market Reports are monthly and use preliminary observed inventory data
caveats: Monthly reporting lags make them useful for trend confirmation, not immediate tracking.

interpretation: European Commission said EU countries are contributing approximately 20% to the IEA release
caveats: This is an approximate share of the IEA collective action, not a finalized EU-only release series.

value: 90 days net imports or 61 days consumption
interpretation: EU law requires member states to maintain emergency oil stocks at the required days-equivalent level
caveats: This is the stockholding obligation, not a discretionary release.

interpretation: EU commercial stocks were 45.7 Mt in May 2025
caveats: Public explainer is lagged; not a post-shock weekly release series.

interpretation: Commercial stocks fell from 52.6 Mt in May 2024 to 45.7 Mt in May 2025
caveats: Year-on-year comparison is a rough baseline/seasonal comparison, not a policy release.

timing: Eurostat explainer latest visible data May 2025
interpretation: Public article lags the underlying databrowser and is not near-real-time
caveats: Use the Commission/IEA updates for post-shock tracking.

timing: Allocated in March 2026; private release began 2026-03-16; national release began 2026-03-26
interpretation: Japan was allocated 79.8 mb under the IEA collective action; private reserves began first, then national reserves
caveats: Public/private mix matters; this is not a pure public strategic draw.

interpretation: METI kept the private oil stockpiling obligation reduced from 70 days to 55 days
caveats: This is a temporary obligation reduction, not an inventory drawdown.

timing: No separate English-language commercial draw series found in the cited METI pages
interpretation: Public English pages here focus on reserve policy rather than monthly commercial inventory series
caveats: The LNG survey was discontinued in April 2022, which is another visibility constraint.

interpretation: METI estimated alternative procurement would cover about 60% of May Hormuz volume and 70% or more in June
caveats: This is an alternative-procurement baseline, not a seasonal inventory change.

timing: METI petroleum statistics page updated 2026-06-30 for May 2026
interpretation: METI petroleum statistics are monthly; next issue was set for 2026-07-31
caveats: Monthly cadence means any official stock picture lags by roughly a month.

interpretation: Korea's contribution to the IEA collective action is 22.5 mb
caveats: Public IEA table does not yet give the detailed split for Korea.

timing: No public release or obligation-relief notice found
interpretation: KNOC public material does not show a quantified private obligation reduction in the sources reviewed
caveats: Do not infer relief from the reserve snapshot.

interpretation: KNOC said South Korea held 99.49 mb of strategic petroleum reserves across nine sites
caveats: This is a stock snapshot, not a draw; no public weekly release series was found.

interpretation: KNOC reported 146 mb of total storage capacity
caveats: Capacity is a buffer metric, not a seasonal change or release.

timing: Latest public ESG material is end-2024 / published 2025
interpretation: No public weekly reserve-release series was found in KNOC's English materials
caveats: This makes Korea the least granular of the five rows here.
